title: U.S. food prices relative to wages
description: The experimental pressure measure publishes food and wage components rather than replacing them with a composite score.
plainLanguageSummary: The experimental pressure measure publishes food and wage components rather than replacing them with a composite score.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Published indices and explicitly labelled derived changes; no interpolation across native frequencies.
calculation.explanation: The experimental pressure measure publishes food and wage components rather than replacing them with a composite score.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: The experimental pressure measure publishes food and wage components rather than replacing them with a composite score.
sourceNotes: Derived from Official U.S. source distributed through Federal Reserve Economic Data
